demo_reading_p: The platform's text will adjust dynamically. This improves readability for all users.
profile_verify_deactivate_note: Your account status will change to 'INACTIVE'
profile_verify_send_note: We will send a security code to:
profile_danger_desc: Your account will be permanently set to INACTIVE status.
profile_danger_final_confirm_msg: PIN validated. By confirming now, your session will close immediately.

seo.title: Security Tips | Local Management
seo.desc: Advanced digital shielding and data protection guide for the congregation.

security.hero_sub: Essential habits for the care of your privacy and personal information.

estafas.title: 1. Cyber Scams
estafas.desc: Fake digital content can mimic known voices and faces to steal your information or money.

tags.prevention_info: If you are rushed to provide money or sensitive data: stop, think, and always verify.
tags.verification_info: End the communication and call the person yourself through a known secure channel.
tags.deepfake_info: Identity cloning technology. Do not trust only what you see or hear through a screen.

physical.title: 2. Physical Perimeter
physical.desc: Your phone is the master key. A device without a lock is an open door to your entire private life.

tags.biometry_info: Set up FaceID or Fingerprint. It is much safer than a pattern that someone could spy over your shoulder.
tags.updates_info: Do not ignore 'New version' messages; they often close critical security holes discovered recently.
tags.encryption_info: Make sure disk encryption is active to protect your files and photos from potential physical theft.

keys.title: 3. High-Impact Keys
keys.desc: Avoid using obvious data. Most attacks occur due to weak passwords or those repeated across multiple sites.

tags.2fa_info: Activate two-step verification on all your accounts. It is the most important second layer of shielding.
tags.robustness_info: Use long phrases instead of simple words. Example: 'Blue-Sky-2026!' is exponentially better than 'peter123'.
tags.passphrases_info: Long phrases are almost impossible for today's attacker computers to crack.

phishing.title: 4. Detecting the Hook
phishing.desc: Criminals pretend to be technical support or banks to steal your data through fear or excessive urgency.

tags.urgency_info: If the message says 'Your account will close in 1 hour if you don't click', it is almost certainly a scam.
tags.links_info: Check the URL carefully. Scammers often use similar names like 'g00gle.net' instead of 'google.com'.
tags.files_info: Never open unexpected attachments, especially if they have extensions like .ZIP, .EXE, or .BAT.

family.title: 5. Children's Legacy
family.desc: Children lack the malice to detect traps. Teach them that what is uploaded to the internet stays there forever.

tags.identity_info: Avoid revealing sensitive data in public photos, such as school uniforms, company logos, or real-time locations.
tags.strangers_info: Golden rule for minors: 'If you don't know them physically in person, they are not a real friend'.
tags.privacy_info: Periodically review the privacy settings of your family members' social networks and devices.

backup.title: 6. Data Survival
backup.desc: A virus can lock or delete your files. The only truly safe copy is the one that is physically disconnected.

tags.frequency_info: Back up your most valuable photos and documents at least once a month.
tags.disconnection_info: Copy your files to an external drive and, most importantly, physically disconnect it from the computer when finished.
tags.cloud_info: Use cloud storage services with encryption, but never rely solely on them as a single copy.
